title: General Intuition in talks to raise $300M at around $2B valuation
slides: General Intuition wants $300M — why the rush?
It builds AI for corporate decision-making without humans
Rivals like Glean raised similar sums with less traction
You now face an AI competitor that learns from your data
Test their demos against your own analysts this quarter
thread_text:
1. Why the $300M rush?

General Intuition wants $300M before proving its AI can replace human analysts.

2. Your data trains it

This AI learns from your company data to make decisions—without humans.

3. Rivals raised more with less

Glean got $260M with almost no revenue. Now General Intuition wants more.

4. You now face that AI

Your own data fuels a competitor you must compete against.

5. Test it this quarter

Run their demo against your analysts. If it beats them, what's your plan?

script: It builds AI for decisions without humans.
Rivals raised similar sums with less traction.
Your data trains your next competitor.

instagram.caption:
Your next competitor is training on your own data. General Intuition, an AI that replaces human analysts, is raising $300M at a $2B valuation—before proving it works fully.

Rivals like Glean raised similar sums with less traction. This AI learns from your company’s data to make corporate decisions 🧠 no human oversight.

If you create business analyses or competitive reports, you now race against an AI fueled by your own inputs 📊

Run their demo against your best analyst this quarter. What happens if it wins?

carousel_slides.description: General Intuition wants $300M to build AI that learns from your company data and replaces human analysts.
This AI makes corporate decisions using your own data—no human oversight needed.
Glean got $260M with almost no revenue. Now General Intuition wants $300M before proving its model.
Your own data fuels a competitor you must compete against—test it before it scales.
Run their demo against your analysts. If it outperforms, prepare your response now.
